title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: r4subcore foundation R4SUB (R Regulatory Submission) ecosystem. defines three responsibilities every R4SUB pillar package depends : Run context — lightweight metadata envelope ties every piece evidence back specific study execution event. Evidence schema — fixed 17-column contract standardises findings heterogeneous sources (validators, parsers, manual checks) stored exchanged. Validation helpers — functions enforce schema every ingestion point downstream scoring profiling can always trust data.

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: run context captures ran assessment, study, environment, time. Every evidence row create stamped run_id study_id context, ensuring full traceability. run_id generated automatically timestamp, can supply need reproducibility tests pipelines.
code: ctx <- r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY001", environment = "DEV") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112310-wl4dieex" print(ctx) #> <r4sub_run_context> #>   run_id:       R4S-20260316112310-wl4dieex  #>   study_id:     STUDY001  #>   environment:  DEV  #>   user:         runner  #>   created_at:   2026-03-16 11:23:10 ctx_custom <- r4sub_run_context(   study_id    = "STUDY001",   environment = "PROD",   run_id      = "RUN-2024-001" ) #> ℹ Run context created: "RUN-2024-001" ctx_custom$run_id #> [1] "RUN-2024-001"

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: evidence schema defines 17 columns every evidence table must contain. Call evidence_schema() inspect contract time:
code: schema <- evidence_schema() # Column names in canonical order names(schema) #>  [1] "run_id"           "study_id"         "asset_type"       "asset_id"         #>  [5] "source_name"      "source_version"   "indicator_id"     "indicator_name"   #>  [9] "indicator_domain" "severity"         "result"           "metric_value"     #> [13] "metric_unit"      "message"          "location"         "evidence_payload" #> [17] "created_at"

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: canon_severity() canon_result() normalise common aliases canonical values accepted schema:

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: as_evidence() main ingestion function. supply data frame contains minimum required columns, pass run context, function: fills run_id study_id context, fills nullable columns appropriately-typed NA, sets created_at current time absent, validates result returning . can inspect resulting evidence table:
code: raw <- data.frame(   asset_type       = "validation",   asset_id         = "ADSL",   source_name      = "pinnacle21",   indicator_id     = "P21-SD0001",   indicator_name   = "Missing variable label",   indicator_domain = "quality",   severity         = "high",   result           = "fail",   message          = "Variable AGEU is missing a label",   location         = "ADSL.AGEU",   metric_value     = 0,   metric_unit      = "score",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ev <- as_evidence(raw, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row # All 17 schema columns are present ncol(ev) #> [1] 17 ev[, c("run_id", "study_id", "indicator_id", "result", "severity")] #>                        run_id study_id indicator_id result severity #> 1 R4S-20260316112310-wl4dieex STUDY001   P21-SD0001   fail     high

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: validate_evidence() runs checks as_evidence() calls internally. Use receive evidence produced externally want confirm meets contract processing:
code: validate_evidence(ev)  # returns TRUE invisibly if everything is valid

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: combining evidence different sources indicators, use bind_evidence(). validates table individually combining, preventing schema violations silently propagating:
code: # A second finding — a passed check on the same dataset raw2 <- data.frame(   asset_type       = "dataset",   asset_id         = "ADSL",   source_name      = "r4subcore",   indicator_id     = "Q-NROW-001",   indicator_name   = "Dataset row count",   indicator_domain = "quality",   severity         = "info",   result           = "pass",   message          = "ADSL has 254 subjects",   metric_value     = 254,   metric_unit      = "count",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev2 <- as_evidence(raw2, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row  combined <- bind_evidence(ev, ev2) #> ✔ Bound 2 evidence tables: 2 total rows nrow(combined) #> [1] 2

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: evidence_summary() aggregates evidence table domain, severity, result, source, giving one-page digest findings:
code: evidence_summary(combined) #>   indicator_domain severity result source_name n #> 1          quality     high   fail  pinnacle21 1 #> 2          quality     info   pass   r4subcore 1

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: Evidence tables can persisted reloaded CSV, RDS, JSON format. exported file retains full schema import_evidence() can re-validate way back . RDS faithful format preserves POSIXct without string-conversion round-trip. JSON useful evidence needs consumed non-R tooling.
code: # Export to CSV tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") export_evidence(combined, file = tmp, format = "csv")  # Import and re-validate ev_reloaded <- import_evidence(tmp, format = "csv") nrow(ev_reloaded)

title: Getting Started with r4subcore
text: evidence table, R4SUB pillar packages consume directly: r4subusability — four usability indicators (label quality, Define-XML completeness, annotation coverage, reviewer guide presence). r4subscore — weighted scoring across domains single submission readiness index. r4subprofile — regulatory authority profile templates (FDA, EMA, PMDA, ANVISA, Health Canada, NMPA) map scores submission requirements.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pawan Rama Mali. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mali P (2026). r4subcore: Core Contracts, Parsers, Scoring Primitives. R package version 0.1.2, https://r4sub.github.io/r4subcore/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {r4subcore: Core Contracts, Parsers, and Scoring Primitives},   author = {Pawan Rama Mali},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.2},   url = {https://r4sub.github.io/r4subcore/}, }

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
text: r4subcore foundational package R4SUB ecosystem. defines core data contracts, parsers, evidence schema, scoring primitives needed quantify clinical submission readiness. intentionally stable: R4SUB packages (r4subtrace, r4subrisk, r4subscore, r4subusability) build structures interfaces.

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
text: Development version:

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
text: inputs normalized single tabular contract — evidence dataset. enables scoring, drilldown, traceability, reporting.

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
code: library(r4subcore)  # Create a run context ctx <- r4sub_run_context(study_id = "ABC123", environment = "DEV") ctx$run_id  # Coerce raw data into the evidence schema ev <- as_evidence(raw_df) validate_evidence(ev)  # Summarize evidence_summary(ev)

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
text: Contract-first: normalize everything evidence rows Transparent scoring: black-box weights; everything configurable Tool-agnostic: supports P21, Define-XML, extensible sources Reproducible: run_id + source_version captured everywhere Composable: small functions, tight coupling

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
text: scope: evidence schema, parsers, indicator interfaces, scoring primitives, run context. scope: full SCI calculation (r4subscore), dashboards (r4subui), traceability logic (r4subtrace), risk quantification (r4subrisk), usability indicators (r4subusability).

title: r4subcore — R4SUB Foundation Layer
text: MIT

title: Aggregate Indicator Scores — aggregate_indicator_score
text: Computes summary scores evidence table, grouped one columns.

title: Aggregate Indicator Scores — aggregate_indicator_score
text: ev valid evidence data.frame. Character vector column names group . Default: c("indicator_id"). method Aggregation method: "mean", "min", "weighted". "weighted" method uses severity_to_weight() result_to_score().

title: Aggregate Indicator Scores — aggregate_indicator_score
text: data.frame grouping columns plus score (0–1) n_evidence (count rows).

title: Aggregate Indicator Scores — aggregate_indicator_score
code: ctx <- suppressMessages(r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV")) ev <- suppressMessages(as_evidence(   data.frame(     asset_type = rep("validation", 3), asset_id = rep("ADSL", 3),     source_name = rep("pinnacle21", 3),     indicator_id = c("SD0001", "SD0001", "SD0002"),     indicator_name = c("SD0001", "SD0001", "SD0002"),     indicator_domain = rep("quality", 3),     severity = c("high", "medium", "low"),     result = c("fail", "warn", "pass"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   ctx = ctx )) aggregate_indicator_score(ev, by = "indicator_id", method = "weighted") #>   indicator_id score n_evidence #> 1       SD0001   0.2          2 #> 2       SD0002   1.0          1

title: Coerce to Evidence Table — as_evidence
text: Takes data.frame coerces valid evidence table. Fills missing nullable columns NA correct type validates controlled vocabulary columns.

title: Coerce to Evidence Table — as_evidence
text: x data.frame (tibble) least required evidence columns. ctx optional r4sub_run_context. provided, run_id study_id filled context missing. ... Additional columns set (e.g., asset_type = "validation").

title: Coerce to Evidence Table — as_evidence
text: data.frame conforming evidence schema.

title: Coerce to Evidence Table — as_evidence
code: ctx <- r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112306-ombeu7fd" df <- data.frame(   asset_type = "validation",   asset_id = "ADSL",   source_name = "pinnacle21",   indicator_id = "P21-001",   indicator_name = "Missing variable",   indicator_domain = "quality",   severity = "high",   result = "fail",   message = "Variable AGEU missing",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev <- as_evidence(df, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row

title: Bind Evidence Tables — bind_evidence
text: Row-binds multiple evidence data.frames validating one.

title: Bind Evidence Tables — bind_evidence
text: ... Evidence data.frames bind.

title: Bind Evidence Tables — bind_evidence
text: single combined evidence data.frame.

title: Bind Evidence Tables — bind_evidence
code: ctx <- suppressMessages(r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV")) make_ev <- function(ind_id) {   suppressMessages(as_evidence(     data.frame(       asset_type = "validation", asset_id = "ADSL",       source_name = "pinnacle21", indicator_id = ind_id,       indicator_name = ind_id, indicator_domain = "quality",       severity = "low", result = "pass",       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     ),     ctx = ctx   )) } ev1 <- make_ev("IND-001") ev2 <- make_ev("IND-002") combined <- suppressMessages(bind_evidence(ev1, ev2)) nrow(combined) #> [1] 2

title: Canonical Result Values — canon_result
text: Maps common result/status labels canonical set: pass, fail, warn, na.

title: Canonical Result Values — canon_result
text: x Character vector result values.

title: Canonical Result Values — canon_result
text: Character vector canonical result labels.

title: Canonical Severity Values — canon_severity
text: Maps common severity labels (case-insensitive) canonical set.

title: Canonical Severity Values — canon_severity
text: x Character vector severity values.

title: Canonical Severity Values — canon_severity
text: Character vector canonical severity labels.

title: Parse Define-XML to Evidence — define_xml_to_evidence
text: Reads Define-XML 2.0/2.1 file extracts dataset, variable, derivation completeness checks standard evidence table format. Three indicators evaluated asset: Q-DEFINE-001 Dataset present non-empty label. Q-DEFINE-002 Variable documented (label dataType). Q-DEFINE-003 Derivation text present derived variables.

title: Parse Define-XML to Evidence — define_xml_to_evidence
text: file Character. Path Define-XML file (.xml). ctx r4sub_run_context providing run study metadata. source_version Character. Version label Define-XML standard. Default: "2.1".

title: Parse Define-XML to Evidence — define_xml_to_evidence
text: data.frame conforming evidence schema, one row per dataset-level check (Q-DEFINE-001), per variable check (Q-DEFINE-002), per derivation check (Q-DEFINE-003).

title: Parse Define-XML to Evidence — define_xml_to_evidence
code: # \donttest{ # Build a minimal Define-XML 2.1 document xml_txt <- '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <ODM xmlns="http://www.cdisc.org/ns/odm/v1.3"      xmlns:def="http://www.cdisc.org/ns/def/v2.1">   <Study OID="STUDY001">     <MetaDataVersion OID="MDV.001" Name="Define-XML 2.1">       <def:ItemGroupDef OID="IG.ADSL" Name="ADSL" SASDatasetName="ADSL"                         Repeating="No" Purpose="Analysis"                         def:Label="Subject-Level Analysis Dataset">         <ItemRef ItemOID="IT.ADSL.USUBJID" Mandatory="Yes"/>         <ItemRef ItemOID="IT.ADSL.AGE" Mandatory="Yes"/>       </def:ItemGroupDef>       <ItemDef OID="IT.ADSL.USUBJID" Name="USUBJID"                DataType="text" Length="20"                SASFieldName="USUBJID">         <Description><TranslatedText>Unique Subject Identifier</TranslatedText></Description>       </ItemDef>       <ItemDef OID="IT.ADSL.AGE" Name="AGE"                DataType="integer" Length="8"                SASFieldName="AGE">         <Description><TranslatedText>Age</TranslatedText></Description>         <def:Origin Type="Derived">           <def:Description><TranslatedText>Derived from RFSTDTC</TranslatedText></def:Description>         </def:Origin>       </ItemDef>     </MetaDataVersion>   </Study> </ODM>' tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") writeLines(xml_txt, tmp) ctx <- r4sub_run_context("STUDY001", "DEV") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112306-vj57qv4a" ev <- define_xml_to_evidence(tmp, ctx) #> ℹ Define-XML: found 0 dataset(s) (ItemGroupDef) #> ℹ Define-XML: found 2 variable definition(s) (ItemDef) #> ! No ItemGroupDef elements found in Define-XML #> ℹ define_xml_to_evidence: 3 rows generated #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows nrow(ev) #> [1] 3 # }

title: Evidence Table Schema Definition — evidence_schema
text: Returns column specification R4SUB evidence table. element describes column's expected R type , applicable, set allowed values.

title: Evidence Table Schema Definition — evidence_schema
text: named list. element list type (character) optionally allowed (character vector) nullable (logical).

title: Evidence Table Schema Definition — evidence_schema
code: str(evidence_schema()) #> List of 17 #>  $ run_id          :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>  $ study_id        :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>  $ asset_type      :List of 3 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>   ..$ allowed : chr [1:6] "dataset" "define" "program" "validation" ... #>  $ asset_id        :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>  $ source_name     :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>  $ source_version  :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi TRUE #>  $ indicator_id    :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>  $ indicator_name  :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>  $ indicator_domain:List of 3 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>   ..$ allowed : chr [1:4] "quality" "trace" "risk" "usability" #>  $ severity        :List of 3 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>   ..$ allowed : chr [1:5] "info" "low" "medium" "high" ... #>  $ result          :List of 3 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE #>   ..$ allowed : chr [1:4] "pass" "fail" "warn" "na" #>  $ metric_value    :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "double" #>   ..$ nullable: logi TRUE #>  $ metric_unit     :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi TRUE #>  $ message         :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi TRUE #>  $ location        :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi TRUE #>  $ evidence_payload:List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "character" #>   ..$ nullable: logi TRUE #>  $ created_at      :List of 2 #>   ..$ type    : chr "POSIXct" #>   ..$ nullable: logi FALSE

title: Summarize Evidence — evidence_summary
text: Returns summary data.frame counts grouped domain, severity, result, source.

title: Summarize Evidence — evidence_summary
text: ev valid evidence data.frame.

title: Summarize Evidence — evidence_summary
text: data.frame columns: indicator_domain, severity, result, source_name, n.

title: Summarize Evidence — evidence_summary
code: ctx <- suppressMessages(r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV")) ev <- suppressMessages(as_evidence(   data.frame(     asset_type = "validation", asset_id = "ADSL",     source_name = "pinnacle21", indicator_id = "SD0001",     indicator_name = "SD0001", indicator_domain = "quality",     severity = "high", result = "fail",     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   ctx = ctx )) evidence_summary(ev) #>   indicator_domain severity result source_name n #> 1          quality     high   fail  pinnacle21 1

what: Export Evidence Table to File — export_evidence
title: Export Evidence Table to File — export_evidence
text: Validates evidence table writes disk requested format. Metadata attributes (exported_at, r4subcore_version, nrow) attached returned path value traceability.

title: Export Evidence Table to File — export_evidence
Export Evidence Table to File — export_evidence
text: evidence valid evidence data.frame (produced as_evidence()). file Character. Destination file path (including extension). format Character. One "csv", "rds", "json". Default: "csv".

title: Export Evidence Table to File — export_evidence
text: Invisibly returns file attributes: exported_at POSIXct timestamp export. r4subcore_version Package version string. nrow Number rows written.

title: Export Evidence Table to File — export_evidence
code: ctx <- suppressMessages(r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV")) ev <- suppressMessages(as_evidence(   data.frame(     asset_type = "validation", asset_id = "ADSL",     source_name = "pinnacle21", indicator_id = "SD0001",     indicator_name = "SD0001", indicator_domain = "quality",     severity = "high", result = "fail",     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   ctx = ctx )) tmp_csv <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") out <- suppressMessages(export_evidence(ev, tmp_csv, format = "csv")) file.exists(out) #> [1] TRUE

what: Generate a Stable Hash ID — hash_id
title: Generate a Stable Hash ID — hash_id
text: Creates deterministic hash one character inputs. Uses MD5 via base R's digest-like approach lightweight, dependency-free implementation.

title: Generate a Stable Hash ID — hash_id
Generate a Stable Hash ID — hash_id
text: ... Character values hash together. Concatenated "|". prefix Optional prefix prepended hash (e.g., "RUN", "IND").

title: Generate a Stable Hash ID — hash_id
text: character string form prefix-hexhash just hexhash.

title: Generate a Stable Hash ID — hash_id

what: Import Evidence Table from File — import_evidence
title: Import Evidence Table from File — import_evidence
text: Reads evidence table previously saved export_evidence(), validates evidence schema.

title: Import Evidence Table from File — import_evidence
Import Evidence Table from File — import_evidence
text: file Character. Path file read. format Character. One "csv", "rds", "json". Default: "csv".

title: Import Evidence Table from File — import_evidence
text: validated evidence data.frame conforming evidence schema.

title: Import Evidence Table from File — import_evidence
code: ctx <- suppressMessages(r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV")) ev <- suppressMessages(as_evidence(   data.frame(     asset_type = "validation", asset_id = "ADSL",     source_name = "pinnacle21", indicator_id = "SD0001",     indicator_name = "SD0001", indicator_domain = "quality",     severity = "high", result = "fail",     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   ctx = ctx )) tmp_rds <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") suppressMessages(export_evidence(ev, tmp_rds, format = "rds")) ev2 <- suppressMessages(import_evidence(tmp_rds, format = "rds")) nrow(ev2) #> [1] 1

what: Safely Serialize to JSON String — json_safely
title: Safely Serialize to JSON String — json_safely
text: Converts R object valid JSON string. Returns "{}" failure NULL/empty inputs.

title: Safely Serialize to JSON String — json_safely
Safely Serialize to JSON String — json_safely
text: x R object serialize.

title: Safely Serialize to JSON String — json_safely
text: single character string containing valid JSON.

title: Safely Serialize to JSON String — json_safely
Normalize to 0–1 Range — normalize_01
text: Applies min-max normalization numeric vector, optionally clamping values [0, 1].

title: Normalize to 0–1 Range — normalize_01
text: x Numeric vector. direction Character. "higher_better" (default) maps max 1; "lower_better" maps min 1. clamp Logical. TRUE, clamp output [0, 1].

title: Normalize to 0–1 Range — normalize_01
text: Numeric vector normalized 0–1.

what: Parse Pinnacle21 Output to Evidence — p21_to_evidence
title: Parse Pinnacle21 Output to Evidence — p21_to_evidence
text: Converts data.frame Pinnacle21-style validation results standard evidence table format. Column names detected case-insensitively.

title: Parse Pinnacle21 Output to Evidence — p21_to_evidence
code: p21_to_evidence(   p21_df,   ctx,   asset_type = "validation",   source_version = NULL,   default_domain = "quality" )

title: Parse Pinnacle21 Output to Evidence — p21_to_evidence
text: p21_df data.frame containing Pinnacle21 validation output. Expected columns (case-insensitive): Rule (Rule ID), Message, Severity, Dataset, Variable, Result (Status). ctx r4sub_run_context providing run study metadata. asset_type Character. Asset type label. Default: "validation". source_version Character NULL. Version P21 tool. default_domain Character. Indicator domain. Default: "quality".

title: Parse Pinnacle21 Output to Evidence — p21_to_evidence
text: data.frame conforming evidence schema.

title: Parse Pinnacle21 Output to Evidence — p21_to_evidence
code: p21_raw <- data.frame(   Rule = c("SD0001", "SD0002"),   Message = c("Missing variable label", "Invalid format"),   Severity = c("Error", "Warning"),   Dataset = c("ADSL", "ADAE"),   Variable = c("AGE", "AESTDTC"),   Status = c("Failed", "Warning"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ctx <- r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112308-6glbznel" ev <- p21_to_evidence(p21_raw, ctx) #> ℹ Parsed 2 rows from Pinnacle21 output #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows

title: Create a Run Context — r4sub_run_context
text: run context captures metadata particular evidence collection run. provides unique run_id, study identifier, environment label, timestamps used throughout evidence ingestion.

title: Create a Run Context — r4sub_run_context
code: r4sub_run_context(   study_id,   environment = c("DEV", "UAT", "PROD"),   user = NULL,   run_id = NULL,   timestamp = Sys.time() )

title: Create a Run Context — r4sub_run_context
text: study_id Character. Study identifier (e.g., "ABC123"). environment Character. One "DEV", "UAT", "PROD". user Character NULL. Username; defaults system user. run_id Character NULL. NULL, unique ID generated. timestamp POSIXct. Defaults current time.

title: Create a Run Context — r4sub_run_context
text: list class r4sub_run_context elements: run_id, study_id, environment, user, created_at.

title: Create a Run Context — r4sub_run_context
code: ctx <- r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY001", environment = "DEV") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112308-qm66mr5e" ctx$run_id #> [1] "R4S-20260316112308-qm66mr5e" ctx$study_id #> [1] "STUDY001"

what: r4subcore: Core Contracts, Parsers, and Scoring Primitives — r4subcore-package
title: r4subcore: Core Contracts, Parsers, and Scoring Primitives — r4subcore-package
text: Foundational package R4SUB (R Regulatory Submission) ecosystem. Defines core evidence table schema, parsers, indicator abstractions, scoring primitives needed quantify clinical submission readiness. Provides standardized contract ingesting heterogeneous sources (validation outputs, metadata, traceability) single evidence framework.

title: r4subcore: Core Contracts, Parsers, and Scoring Primitives — r4subcore-package
text: Maintainer: Pawan Rama Mali prm@outlook.(ORCID) [copyright holder]

title: Register an Indicator — register_indicator
text: Adds indicator definition local -memory registry.

title: Register an Indicator — register_indicator
code: register_indicator(   indicator_id,   domain,   description,   expected_inputs = character(0),   default_thresholds = numeric(0),   tags = character(0) )

title: Register an Indicator — register_indicator
text: indicator_id Character. Stable identifier indicator. domain Character. One "quality", "trace", "risk", "usability". description Character. Human-readable description. expected_inputs Character vector. Evidence source types indicator expects. default_thresholds Named numeric vector. Optional thresholds. tags Character vector. Optional tags (e.g., "define", "adam").

title: Register an Indicator — register_indicator
text: indicator definition list, invisibly.

title: Register an Indicator — register_indicator
code: register_indicator(   indicator_id = "P21-001",   domain = "quality",   description = "Required variable is missing from dataset" ) #> ✔ Registered indicator: "P21-001"

title: Map Severity to Numeric Weight — severity_to_weight
text: Converts canonical severity labels numeric penalty multipliers 0–1 scale.

title: Map Severity to Numeric Weight — severity_to_weight
Map Severity to Numeric Weight — severity_to_weight
text: severity Character vector canonical severity values (info, low, medium, high, critical).

title: Map Severity to Numeric Weight — severity_to_weight
text: Numeric vector weights.

title: Map Severity to Numeric Weight — severity_to_weight
text: Default mapping: info     = 0.00 low      = 0.25 medium   = 0.50 high     = 0.75 critical = 1.00

Validate Evidence Table — validate_evidence
text: Checks data.frame conforms evidence schema. Verifies column presence, types, controlled vocabulary values.

title: Validate Evidence Table — validate_evidence
text: ev data.frame validate.

title: Validate Evidence Table — validate_evidence
text: TRUE invisibly valid; throws error otherwise.

title: Validate Evidence Table — validate_evidence
code: ctx <- suppressMessages(r4sub_run_context("STUDY1", "DEV")) ev <- suppressMessages(as_evidence(   data.frame(     asset_type = "validation", asset_id = "ADSL",     source_name = "pinnacle21", indicator_id = "SD0001",     indicator_name = "SD0001", indicator_domain = "quality",     severity = "high", result = "fail",     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   ctx = ctx )) validate_evidence(ev)

title: Validate Indicator Metadata — validate_indicator
text: Checks indicator definition list well-formed.

title: Validate Indicator Metadata — validate_indicator
text: indicator list required fields: indicator_id, domain, description. Optional fields: expected_inputs, default_thresholds, tags.

title: Validate Indicator Metadata — validate_indicator
text: TRUE invisibly valid; throws error otherwise.

title: Validate Indicator Metadata — validate_indicator
code: validate_indicator(list(   indicator_id = "P21-001",   domain = "quality",   description = "Missing required variable" ))

title: r4subcore 0.1.2
text: define_xml_to_evidence(): Parse Define-XML 2.0/2.1 files evidence, emitting Q-DEFINE-001 (dataset present), Q-DEFINE-002 (variable documented), Q-DEFINE-003 (derivation present) indicators. export_evidence() / import_evidence(): Save reload validated evidence tables CSV, RDS, JSON format metadata attributes.

title: r4subcore 0.1.1
text: Added ORCID package author Authors@R. Added r4subdata Suggests — companion example-data package now CRAN.
